item.title: Black sea bass
item.description: Centropristis striata, a temperate Western Atlantic sea bass in the family Serranidae. Lean, firm, structured white flake with deep ocean flavor and a collagen-dense head and spine that yields a gelatinous broth. Moderate fat. The canonical whole fish for Korean maeuntang, Cantonese steaming, and Mediterranean whole-roasting.

item.title: Flounder
item.description: The flatfish group (flounder, fluke, sole, plaice): asymmetric bottom-dwelling fish with thin, pristine, low-fat white fillets, a very mild sweet flavor, and an unusually large skin surface area per pound. The base fish of sole meunière, gajami preparations, and the Vietnamese and Chinese whole-fried canon.

item.title: Golden pompano
item.description: Flat, deep-bodied jack (Trachinotus) with dense white flesh at 8 to 12% fat, fine pin-bones, and a high usable-meat yield. The fish behind Gulf Coast pompano en papillote and the whole steamed or fried pompano of Cantonese and Vietnamese markets.

item.title: Hairtail (beltfish, galchi)
item.description: Long ribbon-shaped silver marine fish, no scales, with delicate fine-grained white meat that lifts cleanly off a single central spine. The defining fish of Korean galchi jorim and galchi gui, Chinese braised daiyu, and Japanese tachiuo shioyaki.

item.title: Milkfish (bangus)
item.description: Firm white-fleshed aquaculture fish (Chanos chanos) with a rich oily belly-fat pocket and a notorious dense network of fine intermuscular Y-bones. The Philippine national fish, central to daing na bangus, sinigang, and rellenong bangus, and the Indonesian bandeng.

item.title: Porgy (scup)
item.description: Medium-firm white-fleshed sea bream in the Sparidae family, low-to-moderate fat, with a high bone-to-collagen ratio that builds body in broths. The unbranded counterpart to branzino and red snapper, sold whole on ice at Asian and Mediterranean markets.

item.title: Rainbow trout
item.description: Freshwater salmonid (Oncorhynchus mykiss) with fine-grained, pinkish-white flesh and moderate fat that sits between lean white fish and salmon. The pliable thin-boned structure that defines amandine, meunière, and pan-seared trout across French, Creole, and American kitchens.

item.title: Striped bass
item.description: Large meaty moderate-fat white fish with firm crisp-prone skin and a high meat-to-bone ratio. The workhorse of whole-roasting, crispy-skin pan-searing, and the Cantonese whole-steam.

item.title: Yellow croaker
item.description: Small-to-medium drumming fish (Larimichthys polyactis, L. crocea) with velvety, mildly sweet white flesh and a thin, crisp-prone skin. The canonical fish of Korean jogi-gui and gulbi, and of Chinese braised and fried huangyu.

item.title: Daing na bangus
item.description: Butterflied milkfish given a light vinegar cure (a daing) in cane vinegar, crushed garlic, cracked black pepper, and salt for several hours, then patted bone-dry and pan-fried skin-crisp. The acid-garlic marinade firms the dense flesh, brightens it, and tempers the rich oily belly; served for breakfast with garlic fried rice, a fried egg, sliced tomato, and a spiced-vinegar dip.

item.title: Galchi jorim (braised hairtail with radish)
item.description: The Korean braise of cross-cut hairtail (galchi) over thick radish coins in a brick-red yangnyeom sauce. Daikon lines the pot bottom and cooks longest, turning translucent and sweet as it drinks the gochugaru-and-gochujang braising liquid; the delicate beltfish segments rest on top and steam-braise without being moved, so the fine flesh stays in clean flakes off the spine. Soy, gochujang, and an anchovy-kelp stock build the glutamate floor; a splash of rice vinegar and fresh scallion land the finish. The sauce reduces glossy and gets spooned over short-grain rice.

item.title: Maeuntang (Korean spicy fish stew)
item.description: A whole black sea bass sectioned with the head and spine intact, poached into a gochugaru-and-doenjang broth built on anchovy-kelp stock. Radish simmers first to a sweet translucence, the fish goes in only when the broth is set, and the pot finishes with tofu, chili, scallion, and a raw crown of crown daisy or watercress. The carcass is the point: its collagen gives the broth a gelatinous body that fillets cannot. A shared, fiery, umami-deep one-pot meal eaten over rice.

item.title: Pan-fried yellow croaker (jogi-gui)
item.description: Small whole yellow croakers salted and patted bone-dry, lightly dredged in flour, and shallow-fried in moderate oil until the thin skin shatters crisp and the sweet white flesh sets moist on the bone. Flipped once and served as a rice-table fish with a cho-ganjang dip of soy, rice vinegar, scallion, gochugaru, and sesame.

item.title: Pompano en papillote
item.description: Two pompano fillets sealed in a parchment crescent over a butter, shallot, garlic, dry white wine, and lemon base, a small folding of shrimp and lump crab tucked alongside, and an anchovy-deepened sauce, baked hard in a fully preheated oven until the packet puffs and steams the fish in its own rendered fat plus wine. Opened at the table. Pompano's high, clean fat makes the sealed packet nearly foolproof.

item.title: Porgy acqua pazza
item.description: A whole porgy poached in acqua pazza, the Neapolitan "crazy water": a shallow fast pan-broth of blistered cherry tomatoes, smashed garlic, dry white wine, water, and good olive oil, bloomed anchovy and chili for depth, the fish poaching only 8 to 12 minutes while its head and bones lend the liquid body. The broth becomes a light brodo spooned over the fish and mopped with grilled bread.

item.title: Saba shioyaki
item.description: Salt-grilled mackerel: saba fillets cross-hatch scored, salted at the furi-jio rate and rested 25 minutes to draw surface water and firm the flesh through protein denaturation, sake-rinsed, patted dry, then cooked skin-side-up under a roaring broiler until the subcutaneous fat renders and the skin blisters to lacquered crackling and the flesh sets fully through. The teishoku and izakaya canon: an oily blue fish managed entirely by salt and dry radiant heat, plated with cool grated daikon oroshi, a soy pour, and a wedge of citrus.

item.title: Trout amandine
item.description: Flour-dredged trout fillets pan-fried in butter to a golden crust, then sauced with a beurre noisette built in the same pan with sliced almonds toasted to gold, finished with lemon and parsley and a Creole dash of Worcestershire. The French truite amandine as it settled into the New Orleans canon: brown-butter Maillard depth as the umami anchor, lemon as the cutting acid.

item.title: Whole fried flounder with nuoc cham
item.description: A whole flounder, scaled and gutted, cross-scored to the bone on both sides, salted and dried bone-dry, dredged in a thin cornstarch-and-rice-flour coat, and fried in hot oil until the broad flat skin shatters crisp and the sweet white flake lifts off the frame. Served whole over shredded lettuce and a pile of cilantro, mint, and bean sprouts, with nuoc cham poured and dipped. The Vietnamese ca chien whole-fried fish: the flat body is the value play, maximum crisp skin per fish, and the umami and finishing acid both live in the nuoc cham.

item.title: Whole-roasted striped bass with salsa verde
item.description: A scored whole striped bass stuffed with lemon, smashed garlic, fennel fronds, thyme, and parsley, rubbed with olive oil and salt, and roasted at 225°C until the skin crisps and the flesh just sets at 55°C. A sharp green salsa verde of parsley, capers, anchovy, garlic, and lemon is spooned over at the table: the anchovy and capers carry the umami, the lemon and red wine vinegar the finishing acid. One fish, best meat-to-bone ratio, feeds four off a single sheet pan.

item.title: Mala dry-charred Brussels sprouts
item.description: Halved sprouts seared cut-face down until the edges blacken, steamed through, then tossed dry with toasted ground Sichuan peppercorn and chili rather than a wet glaze. The char rebalances the sprouts' sulfur bite into nutty sweetness while the mala finish brings a numbing, fragrant heat.

item.title: Scallion-oil oyster mushrooms with white pepper
item.description: Oyster mushrooms torn and dry-seared until the water cooks off and the edges crisp, then dressed in a hot scallion-and-ginger oil with white pepper and a bright splash of rice vinegar. The dish runs pale, clean, and allium-forward while still carrying the mushrooms' own meaty glutamate.

item.title: Sichuan sticky pork meatballs in doubanjiang glaze
item.description: Bouncy pork meatballs built on salt-solubilized myosin, seared hard for fond, then lacquered in a glaze of fermented broad-bean paste, rock sugar, and soy reduced to a glossy cling. The doubanjiang carries fermented-chili heat and deep glutamate while Chinkiang vinegar off the heat snaps the sweetness back into focus.

item.title: Myosin extraction
item.description: Salt a ground or minced protein, then work it hard in one direction until the mass turns tacky and pulls against the spoon. The salt solubilizes myosin, which shears into aligned strands and cross-links on cooking into the springy, water-holding gel that defines a bouncy meatball, fish ball, or sausage.

item.title: White chocolate
item.description: Confection of cocoa butter, milk solids, and sugar with no cocoa solids. The absence of cocoa mass is what makes it pale, mild, and dairy-forward, and what makes its melting and browning behavior diverge from dark chocolate.

item.title: Ube halaya brown butter sugar cookies
item.description: The brown butter sugar cookie rebuilt around ube halaya: toasted milk solids from the butter meet the caramelized condensed milk of the jam, cornstarch buffers the jam moisture, and the pan-bang trick still cracks a rippled, sugar-shelled violet top.

item.title: Ube halaya thumbprint cookies
item.description: Brown butter shortbread with a coconut-rolled edge and a well of ube halaya baked into the center, then refreshed with a second spoon of cold jam so the violet reads vivid against the toasted crumb. Halaya is already a jam, which makes it the native filling for a thumbprint.

item.title: Ube halaya white chocolate cookies
item.description: A brown butter drop cookie built on ube halaya with pools of white chocolate. The white chocolate is the deliberate partner: cocoa butter and milk solids with no cocoa to compete, so the violet yam stays the headline while the chocolate adds creamy, sweet, browning pockets.

item.title: Cremini mushroom (baby bella, white button)
item.description: Agaricus bisporus at three harvest stages: white button (immature), cremini / baby bella (mid, browned strain), and portobello (fully mature, open cap). Mild and watery raw, deeply savory once the water cooks off and the surface browns.

item.title: Acid-forward green salad with sharp vinaigrette
item.description: A simple bowl of crisp greens dressed in a deliberately sharp shallot-dijon-red-wine vinaigrette emulsified to cling, built to cut richness rather than to be a meal. The vinaigrette runs a higher acid-to-oil ratio than the textbook 3:1 (closer to 2:1) because its job is to reset the palate between bites of seared fish or buttered vegetables, not to coat the leaves in fat. Shallot macerated in the vinegar softens its bite and seasons the whole dressing; dijon emulsifies and adds tang.

item.title: Charred broccoli with garlic-anchovy crunch
item.description: Broccoli seared hard in a dry pan until the florets catch deep char, steam-finished briefly under a lid, then dressed with a crumb of panko fried in olive oil with sliced garlic, anchovy, and chili. The broccoli supplies sweet-bitter brassica char; the crumb supplies the savory, crunchy, glutamate-heavy counterpoint that turns a plain green vegetable into a side with structure. The whole dish runs on dry heat: no boiling, no steaming until the very end, so the broccoli browns instead of going gray and sulfurous.
